channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenyan Woman Honored as Elite US Army Recruiter
item.description: Eve Gatah, a Kenyan-born soldier serving in the United States Army, has been recognized as a top recruiter within the force. Her achievement is attributed to divine favor and hard work, inspiring Keny...

item.title: Kasarani Family Struggles to Bury Relative Due to Unpaid Medical Bills
item.description: Joseph Njoroge died on 28 May at Kenyatta University Referral Hospital after being admitted in March following a stroke, leaving behind a KSh 1.7 million medical bill. His family has been unable to gi...

item.title: Balochistan Chief Minister Announces 75 Terrorists Killed in Recent Operations
item.description: Balochistan Chief Minister Sarfraz Bugti announced that 75 terrorists have been killed in intelligence-based operations across the province since July 5. He highlighted the success of Operation Shaban...

item.title: Wildfire forces evacuation near small B.C. highway community as residents monitor blaze
item.description: A wildfire has erupted near a small highway community in Vancouver's East End, prompting local residents to observe the rapidly spreading flames. The incident, which occurred on July 10, 2026, has rai...

item.title: Joburg Music and Comedy Events This Weekend
item.description: This weekend, Johannesburg will host a variety of entertainment events featuring top local artists. Thandiswa Mazwai will perform at The Soirée: Courtside Couture event in Melrose Park on Saturday, Ju...

item.title: Tinubu unveils infrastructure plan to revamp Wasa as economic hub
item.description: President Bola Tinubu announced plans to upgrade Wasa District into a modern commercial hub in Abuja's Federal Capital Territory (FCT) by providing premium infrastructure. During an inauguration event...

item.title: Public prefers Count Binface over Nigel Farage in Clacton by-election poll
item.description: A new poll reveals that 33% of British adults would prefer Count Binface to win the Clacton by-election, compared to 21% for Nigel Farage. The survey highlights growing public scrutiny of Farage’s fin...

item.title: Unclaimed Airstrikes Target Iran Amid Ongoing Regional Tensions
item.description: Unclaimed airstrikes targeting Iran have raised questions about their origin, occurring days after the U.S. concluded its attacks. The strikes, reported by Iranian media, hit southern regions includin...

item.title: Kristi Noem's Divorce Proceedings Revealed During Family Birthday Party Amid Allegations of Husband's Fetish Activities
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem's divorce proceedings were revealed during a family birthday party in South Dakota, according to her mother Corinne Arnold. The event, described as 'excruciatin...

item.title: Stratbase: Upholding the Arbitral Win as a National Responsibility
item.description: The Stratbase Institute emphasized that the 10th anniversary of the landmark arbitral ruling over the West Philippine Sea (WPS) is not merely a celebration of past legal victory but a call to action. ...

item.title: KP Government Withdraws Controversial Clauses from Law Granting Expanded Privileges to Lawmakers
item.description: The Khyber Pakhtunkhwa government has decided to withdraw controversial clauses from the KP Provincial Assembly (Powers, Immunities and Privileges) Act, 2026, following widespread public and media bac...

item.title: Shopify updates policy to ban all vape products due to legal restrictions
item.description: Shopify, the Canadian e-commerce platform, has instructed merchants using its services to remove all vape products from their online stores by July 8. This decision follows pressure from a coalition o...

item.title: Dubai and Tanzania Strengthen Tourism and Business Ties
item.description: Dubai has identified Tanzania as a key strategic market in Africa, focusing on boosting tourism and business partnerships. The collaboration was highlighted during a tourism roadshow in Dar es Salaam,...

item.title: Limpopo man faces harassment by March and March group in Pretoria
item.description: Benjamin Moafrica Wa Maila Maila, a Pedi man from Limpopo, alleges he was harassed by members of the March and March movement in Pretoria. While returning to his car after buying coffee, he was approa...

item.title: Tolentino Defends Philippine Sovereignty Over Batanes Against Chinese Claims
item.description: Labor Secretary Francis Tolentino has strongly rejected claims by Chinese academics asserting that the Batanes Islands belong to China through Taiwan. The Chinese academics, in a report by Jinan Unive...

item.title: Passenger partially sucked out of aircraft after window detachment during flight
item.description: A Ryanair flight from Greece to Germany experienced a mid-air incident when a window detached, partially sucking a passenger out of the cabin. The passenger, a 61-year-old man from Serbia, was held by...

item.title: Police arrest suspects linked to Thato Molosankwe murder and alleged crime syndicate
item.description: South African police have arrested two men connected to the murder of anti-crime activist Thato Molosankwe, revealing ties to a wider criminal syndicate. The arrests, announced by Acting North West Po...

item.title: NSC Condemns China's Claims on Batanes, Emphasizes Sovereignty
item.description: The National Security Council (NSC) has issued a strong warning against Chinese scholars' claims that Batanes is an extension of Taiwan, calling such statements 'baseless' and a threat to national sec...

item.title: NANS Announces Nationwide Protest Against South African Companies Over Alleged Mistreatment of Nigerians
item.description: The National Association of Nigerian Students (NANS) has announced plans for a nationwide protest targeting South African-linked businesses like MTN, MultiChoice, and Stanbic IBTC. The protest is in r...

item.title: Zanzibar Launches Initiative to Protect Children from Online Risks
item.description: Zanzibar has initiated a nationwide campaign to safeguard children from online abuse and exploitation, addressing rising internet usage among youth. The campaign, launched on July 9, 2026, aims to rai...
